scenario: Verdant Healthcare runs a Camunda 8.8 deployment for 6 .NET teams and wants to pick a C# client that aligns with the best-practice documentation before writing 4 new job workers. The lead architect is checking which C# library is featured in the Writing good workers example.
question: Which C# client does the Camunda 8.8 Writing good workers best practice feature as its example?

options.text: The Camunda C# client published under github.com/camunda/camunda-platform-get-started/tree/master/csharp is the example library used in the Writing good workers best practice for showing job worker code in C# for Camunda 8.8

A vendor-maintained F# library is the example for .NET workers in Writing good workers; C# is described in the best practice as awaiting first-class client support in the 8.8 release of Camunda 8 platform
A community-maintained gRPC stub generated from the Zeebe proto files is the example used for C#; the Writing good workers page does not link to any first-party .NET client library for Camunda 8.8
The Camunda 7 External Task Client for .NET is the example C# implementation used in Writing good workers for Camunda 8.8; 8.8 documentation reuses the 7.x .NET client for backwards compatibility

a.text: Correct. The C# section of Writing good workers links to the C# client at github.com/camunda/camunda-platform-get-started/tree/master/csharp and shows how worker code in C# is written against it for Camunda 8.8.

b.text: Incorrect. The best practice features a C# client, not an F# library, and does not describe C# as awaiting first-class support in 8.8.

c.text: Incorrect. The page links to a first-party C# client in the get-started repository, not a community gRPC stub.

d.text: Incorrect. The example is the Camunda 8 C# client, not the Camunda 7 External Task Client for .NET.

explanation: For .NET teams the Camunda 8.8 Writing good workers best practice uses the C# client at github.com/camunda/camunda-platform-get-started/tree/master/csharp as the worked example.
